foundation.title: ARRAYLIST
foundation.subtitle: Dynamic Storage
foundation.content: An ArrayList is the most standard Java way to store lists of objects. Unlike regular arrays, it can shrink or grow as needed.
foundation.scenario: A Growing Backpack. You can put things in (add), move them (remove), and see them (get) as you go. No fixed size.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Building an ArrayList using the generic <Type> syntax.

examples.title: Addition
examples.explanation: Adding a new element to the end of your storage stream.

examples.title: Removal
examples.explanation: Erasing an item by its specific index (e.g. 0 for first).

foundation.keyPoint: ArrayList is part of java.util. Collections API.

foundation.title: MAP
foundation.subtitle: The HashMap
foundation.content: A HashMap maps 'Keys' to 'Values'. Like a real dictionary, you look up a specific 'Word' (Key) to find its 'Definition' (Value) instantly.
foundation.scenario: A Contact Phone Book. You have a huge list of names (Keys). You use the specific name to find the Number (Value).

examples.title: The Put
examples.explanation: Connecting a key (the name) to a value (the number) in memory.

examples.title: The Get
examples.explanation: Using the key to retrieve exactly the data you need.

examples.title: The Check
examples.explanation: Checking if a key even exists in the map to avoid crashes.

foundation.keyPoint: HashMap uses Key:Value. Extremely fast lookup (O(1)).

foundation.title: SET
foundation.subtitle: Unique Bags
foundation.content: A Set is a collection that CANNOT have duplicate items. If you add the same thing twice, it's ignored. Everything is unique.
foundation.scenario: A Guest List for a VIP Party. Once your name is on the list, you can't be added again. You are either ON it once or NOT ON it.

examples.title: Double Add
examples.explanation: Adding the same string twice; Java will silently ignore the second one.

examples.title: Search
examples.explanation: Checking if a value already exists inside the uniqe bag.

examples.title: Size Reveal
examples.explanation: Counting how many unique items are currently inside the set.

foundation.keyPoint: HashSet = Unordered collection of unique objects.
